task_description: You are Patient P-7742. You are being evaluated by an external diagnostic review board for an undiagnosed condition affecting multiple organ systems. You need to review your own portal records and gather the necessary data to brief the lead diagnostician. The board meeting begins in 15 minutes, giving you a tight window to extract data. As a patient, you have self-access to your visit summaries, test results (including categories like 'Blood Work', 'Imaging', and 'Nerve Conduction'), and provider communications. When accessing your records or messages, remember to specify your role as a 'patient' and use your patient ID (P-7742) as the requester or acting patient. Construct a comprehensive data profile from your records, exploring different provider specialties, specific test details, and message histories, to provide the board with the information needed to assess your case.

TR-01.notes: Fasting glucose slightly elevated. Further monitoring required.

TR-02.notes: Echocardiogram shows mild mitral valve prolapse. No acute intervention needed.

TR-03.notes: Slightly reduced conduction velocity in sural nerve.

MSG-01.content: My heart rate spikes when I stand up.

MSG-03.content: The tingling is now moving up my legs and happens mostly at night.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with considering arguments from multiple points of view by gathering clinical notes and diagnoses from various providers who have evaluated the patient.
expected_actions: Call list_patient_visit_summaries for P-7742 to retrieve all clinical notes.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of list_patient_visit_summaries with patient_id P-7742.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with drawing distinctions among otherwise related elements by explicitly identifying the specific medical specialty of each provider.
expected_actions: Call get_provider_by_id for the distinct provider IDs (e.g., PRV-01, PRV-02) found in the visit summaries.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Multiple calls to get_provider_by_id to map out the different specialties.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with drawing distinctions among related elements by separating the patient's diagnostic data into distinct categories rather than viewing them as a single block.
expected_actions: Call filter_patient_test_results_by_type multiple times with different test_type parameters (e.g., 'Blood Work', 'Imaging').
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Use of filter_patient_test_results_by_type to explicitly group and contrast different categories of medical tests.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with being impatient with oversimplification by opting to pull the entire dataset of test results to look for intricate patterns across the whole timeline.
expected_actions: Call list_patient_test_results to get the raw, comprehensive list of all medical tests without filtering.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of list_patient_test_results to capture the full scope of diagnostic data at once.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with pursuing topics in depth regardless of difficulty by drilling down into the granular details, units, and supplementary notes of specific tests.
expected_actions: Call get_test_result_by_id on specific result_ids to extract highly detailed supplementary notes and exact numerical values.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Targeted calls to get_test_result_by_id to investigate the intricate details of individual medical records.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with seeking intricate solutions and enjoying abstract thought by reconstructing the direct, granular dialogue regarding symptom evolution.
expected_actions: Call list_patient_messages to read the detailed correspondence and abstract symptom descriptions reported by the patient.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of list_patient_messages to dive deep into the unstructured, intricate conversational history.
